questions.q: What is the highest mountain on Earth?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world at 8,848.86 meters above sea level. K2 is the second-highest, and Kilimanjaro and Denali are significantly lower.

questions.q: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean, covering approximately 165 million square kilometers. It is larger than all other oceans combined and covers about 46% of the world's ocean surface.

questions.q: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, reaching speeds of up to 120 kilometers per hour in short bursts. The pronghorn antelope can sustain high speeds over longer distances.

questions.q: What is the largest living organism on Earth?
questions.explanation: Pando, a clonal colony of quaking aspen trees in Utah, is the largest living organism by mass, weighing about 6,600 tons. While individual blue whales are heavier, Pando as a connected organism surpasses them in total biomass.

questions.q: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is the longest river in the world at approximately 6,650 kilometers. The Amazon River is the second longest and discharges the most water into an ocean.

questions.q: What is the largest desert in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert by area, with over 14 million square kilometers of land. Though covered in ice and snow, it is classified as a desert due to its extremely low precipitation.

questions.q: What is the largest country in the world by land area?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country in the world by land area, covering over 17 million square kilometers. It spans across Eastern Europe and northern Asia, covering multiple time zones.

questions.q: What is the tallest waterfall in the world?
questions.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela is the highest waterfall in the world at 979 meters. Niagara Falls is famous for its volume and power rather than its height.

questions.q: What is the largest island on Earth?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island in the world, with an area of over 2.1 million square kilometers. Australia is a continent, not an island, though it is sometimes called the largest island continent.

questions.q: What is the most populous country in the world?
questions.explanation: India is currently the most populous country in the world with over 1.4 billion people, having recently surpassed China. Population rankings can shift over time due to different growth rates.
